perturbation.original_text: 16. NATURE OF RELATIONSHIP. The parties expressly acknowledge and agree that SRP is acting as an independent contractor and not as a employee of, or partner or joint venturer with, Logan's.
perturbation.changed_text: 16. NATURE OF RELATIONSHIP. The parties expressly acknowledge and agree that SRP is acting as an independent contractor, but shall be considered an employee for the purposes of liability insurance coverage and worker's compensation.
perturbation.explanation: By stating SRP is an independent contractor but also an employee for specific purposes (liability and worker's compensation), it creates a contradiction. An independent contractor is, by definition, not an employee. This attempted hybrid status introduces ambiguity and potential conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Tennessee Code Annotated, Title 50, Chapter 6 (Worker's Compensation)
perturbation.law_explanation: Tennessee law defines 'employee' and 'independent contractor' distinctly. The modified text attempts to blur this line, potentially creating confusion about worker's compensation responsibilities and whether SRP is covered under Logan's policy, contradicting the clear definitions provided by the statute.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Tennessee Code § 50-6-106 (2021) - Employments Not Covered :: 2021 Tennessee Code :: U.S. Codes and Statutes :: U.S. Law :: Justia There Is a Newer Version of this Section 2024 Tennessee Code 2023 Tennessee Code 2021 Tennessee Code (here) View All Versions View Our Newest Version Here 2021 Tennessee Code Title 50 - Employer and Employee Chapter 6 - Workers' Compensation Law Part 1 - General Provisions § 50-6-106. Employments Not Covered Universal Citation: TN Code § 50-6-106 (2021) Learn more This media-neutral citation is based on the American Association of Law Libraries Universal Citation Guide and is not necessarily the official citation. Previous Next This chapter shall not apply to: Any common carrier doing an interstate business while engaged in interstate commerce, which common carrier and the interstate business are already regulated as to employer's liability or workers' compensation by act of congress, it being the purpose of this law to regulate all such business that the congress has not regulated in the exercise of its jurisdiction to regulate interstate commerce; provided, that this chapter shall apply to those employees of the common carriers with respect to whom a rule of liability is not provided by act of congress; provided, further, that no common carrier by motor vehicle operating pursuant to a certificate of public convenience and necessity shall be deemed the employer of a leased-operator or owner-operator of a motor vehicle or vehicles under a contract to such a common carrier; Notwithstanding subdivision (1)(A), a leased operator or a leased owner/operator of a motor vehicle under contract to a common carrier may elect to be covered under any policy of workers' compensation insurance insuring the common carrier upon written agreement of the common carrier, by filing written notice of the contract, on a form prescribed by the administrator, with the bureau; provided, that the election shall in no way terminate or affect the independent contractor status of the leased
perturbation.justification: The modified text attempts to define SRP as both an independent contractor and an employee for the purposes of liability insurance coverage and worker's compensation, which creates a legal contradiction. Tennessee Code § 50-6-106 addresses worker's compensation and independent contractor status, specifically mentioning leased operators. By attempting to grant employee status for limited purposes, the contract introduces ambiguity concerning worker's compensation coverage and conflicts with the established definition of 'independent contractor'.

perturbation.original_text: 13. INSURANCE. SRP shall provide at its expense, and maintain throughout the term of this Agreement, comprehensive general liability insurance in the amount of Five Million Dollars and No/100's ($5,000,000.00) per occurrence with respect to any liability relating to the activities of SRP in the performance of this Agreement SRP shall supply Logan's with a copy of the Certificate of Insurance naming Logan's as an additional insured.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. INSURANCE. SRP shall provide at its expense, and maintain throughout the term of this Agreement, general liability insurance, to be determined at SRP's discretion, with respect to any liability relating to the activities of SRP in the performance of this Agreement. SRP shall supply Logan's with a copy of the Certificate of Insurance naming Logan's as an additional insured, only if such insurance exists.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies a minimum insurance amount of $5,000,000. The modified text removes this fixed amount, allowing SRP to determine the insurance coverage at its discretion and whether or not to obtain one. The added 'only if such insurance exists' further undermines the requirement.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Tennessee Code Annotated, Title 56, Chapter 1 (Insurance Code - General Provisions)
perturbation.law_explanation: While this section doesn't mandate insurance coverage for all businesses, altering the contract in this way creates potential conflict if Logan's relies on SRP maintaining a specific level of insurance. This creates ambiguity about the levels of insurance in place, if any, contrary to what a reasonable business agreement would imply. Also, SRP is still obligated to comply with other statutory insurance requirements based on its activities.
perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates a specific insurance amount of $5,000,000, which provides a clear and enforceable obligation. The changed text removes this fixed amount and allows SRP to determine the insurance coverage at its discretion, potentially resulting in no insurance at all. The added phrase 'only if such insurance exists' further weakens the obligation. Although no specific law snippet is provided to mandate insurance, the contract initially established a requirement and then removed it, undermining the original intent and potentially exposing Logan's to uncovered liabilities. This alteration creates an ambiguity in the insurance coverage, contradicting the reasonable expectation of a business agreement to provide clear terms, as noted in the Law Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: 10. SPECIAL RIGHT OF TERMINATION. Either party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement, subject to the following terms and conditions: (a) Either party ("the Terminating Party") may terminate this Agreement upon prior written notice to the other party (the "Defaulting Party") upon the occurrence of any of the following conditions: (i) In the event that such party materially breaches this Agreement or materially defaults in the performance of any obligation hereunder and fails to cure said breach within 30 days of written notice to the Defaulting Party by the Terminating Party
perturbation.changed_text: 10. SPECIAL RIGHT OF TERMINATION. Either party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement, subject to the following terms and conditions: (a) Either party ("the Terminating Party") may terminate this Agreement upon prior written notice to the other party (the "Defaulting Party") upon the occurrence of any of the following conditions: (i) In the event that such party breaches this Agreement or defaults in the performance of any obligation hereunder and fails to address said breach within 30 days of written notice to the Defaulting Party by the Terminating Party
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies a 'material breach,' which has a specific legal meaning (a significant breach that goes to the heart of the agreement). By removing the word 'material,' any breach, no matter how minor, could be grounds for termination. This creates uncertainty and contradicts the common law understanding of contract breaches.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Tennessee Uniform Commercial Code, specifically regarding contract performance and breach.
perturbation.law_explanation: While this section doesn't directly define 'material breach,' the UCC (which Tennessee has adopted) implies that not every breach justifies termination. Removing 'material' allows termination for trivial reasons, which is inconsistent with the implied duty of good faith and fair dealing present in contract law and the intention of specifying a 'material' default.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-106. Definitions: "Contract"; "Agreement"; "Contract for sale"; "Sale"; "Present sale"; "Conforming" to Contract; "Termination"; "Cancellation". | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-106. Definitions: "Contract"; "Agreement"; "Contract for sale"; "Sale"; "Present sale"; "Conforming" to Contract; "Termination"; "Cancellation". (1) In this Article unless the context otherwise requires " contract " and " agreement " are limited to those relating to the present or future sale of goods . " Contract for sale " includes both a present sale of goods and a contract to sell goods at a future time. A " sale " consists in the passing of title from the seller to the buyer for a price (Section 2-401 ). A " present sale " means a sale which is accomplished by the making of the contract. (2) Goods or conduct including any part of a performance are " conforming " or conform to the contract when they are in accordance with the obligations under the contract. (3) " Termination " occurs when either party pursuant to a power created by agreement or law puts an end to the contract otherwise than for its breach. On "termination" all obligations which are still executory on both sides are discharged but any right based on prior breach or performance survives. (4) " Cancellation " occurs when either party puts an end to the contract for breach by the other and its effect is the same as that of "termination" except that the cancelling party also retains any remedy for breach of the whole contract or any unperformed balance. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the word 'material' from 'material breach', now allowing termination for any breach, no matter how small. This contradicts the concept of 'termination' as defined in Scraped Law Snippet 1, which states 'Termination occurs when either party pursuant to a power created by agreement or law puts an end to the contract otherwise than for its breach.' While seemingly contradictory at first, it must be read in conjunction with the next line: 'On "termination" all obligations which are still executory on both sides are discharged but any right based on prior breach or performance survives'. If any breach can now lead to termination, this impacts the nature of the contract and goes against its underlying spirit, creating a contradiction. Removing 'material' broadens the scope of what triggers termination, contradicting the intent of specifying 'material' default.
